title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 13 13

questions.question: If not allowed in CRAKE it becomes sweet dish but when brought back in UNDO it appears circle. What is it?
questions.explanation: Option (a) If 'A' is not allowed in CRAKE, it becomes CRKE. So, the meaningful word so formed is RECK, and RECK is not a sweet dish. When brought back in UNDO, it becomes AUNDO. Thus, no meaningful word is formed. Option (b) If 'E' is not allowed in CRAKE, it becomes CRAK. So, the meaningful words so formed are CARK and RACK. RACK and CARK are not a sweet dish. When brought back in UNDO, it becomes EUNDO. Thus, no meaningful word is formed. Option (c) If 'R' is not allowed in CRAKE, it becomes CAKE. And CAKE is a sweet dish. When brought back in UNDO, it becomes RUNDO. Thus, the meaningful word is ROUND which means circle. Option (d) If 'K' is not allowed in CRAKE, it becomes CRAE. So, the meaningful words so formed are ACER, ACRE, CARE, RACE. And these words are not a sweet dish. When brought back in UNDO, it becomes KUNDO. Thus, no meaningful word is formed.

questions.question: Which word comes third when arranged in alphabetical order? telegraph, teleport, telepathy, telephony, telescope
questions.explanation: Sorted order → telegraph telepathy telephony teleport telescope Therefore, the third word is telephony.

questions.question: Read the given information and answer the question that follows: In a certain code language, 'glass reflects light' is written as 'av jd bk', 'light have colours' is written as 'mo zy jd' and 'colours in glass' is written as 'cf av mo'. How will 'reflects' be written in that language?
questions.explanation: It is given that, 'glass reflects light' is written as 'av jd bk' ----(I) 'light have colours' is written as 'mo zy jd' -----(II) 'colours in glass' is written as 'cf av mo' ------(III) From I and III, glass = av From I and II, light = jd From II and III, colours = mo From I, reflects = bk From II, have = zy From III, in = cf

questions.question: In a certain code language: 'guitar piano violin' => 'g6 p2 v5' 'piano cello flute' => 'p2 c4 f5' 'flute guitar trumpet' => 'f5 g6 t7' How is 'trumpet' written in that language?
questions.explanation: 1) Comparing the second and third phrases, the common word is 'flute' and the common code is 'f5'. So, flute = f5. 2) Comparing the first and third phrases, the common word is 'guitar' and the common code is 'g#'. So, guitar = g6. 3) In the third phrase, the only remaining word is 'trumpet', and the only remaining code is 't7'. Therefore, trumpet = t7.

questions.question: In an admission line, Rina is 12th from the front and 9th from the back How many candidates are there in the line?
questions.explanation: Rina is counted once in both 'from front' and 'from back' Total candidates = 12 + 9 − 1 = 20 So, there are 20 candidates in the line.

questions.question: The following question is based on the figures given below which represent different positions of the same dice: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er250819im001.png) Which of the following number is opposite to 1?
questions.explanation: 1 is opposite to 6 2 is opposite to 4 5 is opposite to 3 ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er250819im002.png)

questions.question: What is (16² + 4 x 8) mod 5?
questions.explanation: (16² + 8 x 4) mod 5 = (256 + 32) mod 5 = 288 mod 5 When we divide 288 by 5, we get 3 as a remainder. Hence, 288 mod 5 = 3

questions.question: Introducing a boy, a girl said, "He is the son of the only daughter of my mother's father-in-law". How is that boy related to a girl?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21041/image019.png) That Boy is the cousin of a girl.

questions.question: Words given on the left side of (::) are related with each other by some Logic/Rule/Relation. Select the missing word/word pair on the right side of (::) from the given alternatives based on the same Logic/Rule/Relation. Wood : Hacksaw : Furniture :: ?
questions.explanation: For making furniture, we require hacksaw to cut wood. After cutting, a carpenter makes furniture. Similarly, for making garland, we need to cultivate flowers.

questions.question: Which of the following do you agree with?
options: People should get married only with people of the same religion.
People should put employment of the children before their education.
People should treat everyone equally irrespective of their gender.
People should not intervene in incidents where a husband beats his wife.
questions.explanation: People should treat everyone equally irrespective of their gender and irrespective of their castes, religions, opinions, etc. Marriage should be a choice and should not be bound by religion. Children should always be educated (at least) till they are 14 years old and people should speak against (as well as report any incident of) domestic violence.

questions.question: You are a volunteer at a public vaccination camp. A local dignitary's aide demands that their group be moved to the front, ahead of elderly people in the queue. Security looks to you for guidance. What should you do?
options: Allow them to go first to avoid a scene; it will be quick.
Offer a separate counter by diverting doses from the main line to keep both moving.
Explain the priority protocol (elderly, vulnerable, pre-registered) and refuse to alter medically justified order; involve the supervisor if pressure continues.
Ask the elderly to wait ten minutes and let the dignitary's group through "just this once."
questions.explanation: Health prioritization is policy- and ethics-driven. Explaining and holding the line protects the vulnerable and staff integrity. Queue-jumping, even briefly or via diverted doses, compromises fairness and may risk supply planning.

questions.question: In the question given below, select the option which can replace the question mark (?) in the given series. 23, 128, 46, 64, ?, 32, 184, 16
questions.explanation: This is an alternating series. 23 x 2 = 46 46 x 2 = 92 92 x 2 = 184 128/2 = 64 64/2 = 32 32/2 = 16

questions.question: Madhu distributed 1096 almonds among 120 children out of which 64 are girls. If each girl received 4 more almonds than each boy, then how many almonds did each girl receive?
questions.explanation: Total children = 120 Number of girls = 64 Number of boys = 120 - 64 = 56 Let almonds received by each boy = n Then, almonds received by each girl = n + 4 Now, 1096 = 56n + 64 * (n + 4) 1096 = 56n + 64n + 256 120n = 840 n = 7 Therefore, almonds received by each girl = n + 4 = 7 + 4 = 11

questions.question: In a certain language, if 3 and 9 are interchanged and 6 and 8 are interchanged, then which of the following numbers will be the second highest?
questions.explanation: 7928 will be 7326 3647 will be 9847 8534 will be 6594 6932 will be 8392 So, in ascending order: 6594 < 7326 < 8392 < 9847 Hence, second highest is 8392 i.e., 6932.

questions.question: In a certain code language "VETERAN" is written as "22520518114", then how will "MINUS" be written as ?
questions.explanation: VETERAN 22520518114 MINUS 139142119 The "MINUS" will be written as "139142119".

questions.question: Find the odd one out. T-400, P-256, S-361, Q-324
questions.explanation: The rank of letters in alphabetical series of T, P, S and Q is 20, 16, 19 and 17 respectively. T-------- (20)² = 400 P-------- (16)² = 256 S-------- (19)² = 361 Q-------- (17)² = 289

questions.question: Find the missing term in the given series: a b c _ a b _ c _ a b c a b c c
questions.explanation: The pattern is abcc abbc aabc abcc.. Hence the missing letters are c, b, a.

questions.question: Complete the given series: JKMP, QRTW, XYAD, EFHK, ?
questions.explanation: There is a gap of 0, 1, 2 alphabets respectively between the consecutive alphabets in each group. JK_M_ _ PQR_ T_ _ WXY_A_ _DEF_ H_ _KLM_O_ _ R

questions.question: Based on the following statements, which is the correct conclusion drawn? Statements: All pretty is beautiful. No ugly is beautiful.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p18392/image023.jpg) All pretty is beautiful and no ugly is beautiful. Thus, no pretty is ugly or no ugly is pretty. So, option (a) and (d) are incorrect while option (c) is correct. All pretty is beautiful. Thus, there is a possibility that all beautiful is pretty. So, option (b) is incorrect.

questions.question: If 42 Y 15 Z 6 = 8.8 and 36 Y 6 Z 2 = 8, then 25 Y 4 Z 9 = ?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the statements. Statements: All Sofa are Bed No Bed is chair Some Chair are Table Conclusions: I: Some Tables are not Bed II: Some Bed are Sofa III: Some Chairs are Sofa
questions.explanation: Venn Diagram: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25944/image001.png) Conclusions: I: Some Tables are not Bed ----- as it is clear from the venn diagram that No chair is Bed and Some Table are Chair---- thus is follows II: Some Bed are Sofa ---- Follow because All Sofa are bed is mentioned III: Some Chairs are Sofa ----- Does not follow because All Sofa are Bed, No Bed is chair So, both I and II follow.

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13- Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) (100, 250, 400) (36, 53.5, 71)
questions.explanation: Middle number is average of first and last number. (100+400)/2= 250 (36 + 71)/2 = 53.5 Similarly, (49+81)/2= 130/2= 65 Rest of the options do not follow the same logic.

questions.question: If 'A1B' means 'A is the father of B', 'A3B' means 'A is the mother of B', 'A2B' means 'A is the sister of B', 'A4B' means 'A is the husband of B' and 'A5B' means 'A is the wife of B'. In the expression 'V4Z3Q5U1X2W4Y', how is W related to V?
questions.explanation: V4Z: V is the husband of Z Z3Q: Z is the mother of Q Q5U: Q is the wife of U U1X: U is the father of X X2W: X is the sister of W W4Y: W is the husband of Y ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26066/image016.png) So, W is the grandson of V. Thus, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Pointing to a person in a meeting, a man, Shailja said, "He is the only son of my mother's father's only son". If Shailja is a female, then how is Shailja's mother related to that man's father?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26029/image004.png) So, Shailja's mother is the sister of that man's father.

questions.question: Find the missing term in the following series. 230, 234, 246, 266, 294, 330, ?

Match the Act/Report with its primary focus: Act/ReportPrimary Focus (i) Rowlatt Act(a) Arrest and detention without trial (ii) Nehru Report(b) Blueprint for future Constitution (iii) Simon Commission(c) Constitutional reforms for India (iv) Vernacular Press Act(d) Restrictions on press and sedition
options: i → a, ii → b, iii → c, iv → d
i → c, ii → b, iii → a, iv → d
i → b, ii → a, iii → c, iv → d
i → a, ii → c, iii → b, iv → d
questions.explanation: · The Rowlatt Act of 1919 allowed for the arrest and detention of people without trial on the basis of suspicion. · The Nehru Report of 1928 was a blueprint for the future Constitution of India. · The Simon Commission was appointed to inquire into and report on the working of the system of government introduced by the Act of 1919, which dealt with constitutional reforms for India. · Vernacular Press Act (1878): Imposed restrictions on Indian-language newspapers

questions.question: Which of the following are jawless vertebrates characterised by having an elongated eel-like body, circular mouth, slimy skin and are scaleless?
questions.explanation: Cyclostomes (Class Cyclostomata) are jawless vertebrates characterized by having an elongated eel-like body, a circular suctorial mouth without jaws, slimy skin, and the absence of scales. This group includes hagfishes and lampreys. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Pisces: This term traditionally refers to fishes (which are jawed vertebrates). They typically have scales, paired fins, and gills. (c) Amphibia: Amphibians (like frogs, salamanders) are vertebrates that typically have a larval aquatic stage and an adult terrestrial stage. They have jaws and moist, glandular skin, usually without scales (though some extinct forms had scales). (d) Reptilia: Reptiles (like snakes, lizards, turtles, crocodiles) are vertebrates characterized by scaly skin, breathing air with lungs, and usually laying amniotic eggs. They have jaws. Further insights: Cyclostomes are considered among the most primitive living vertebrates. They belong to the superclass Agnatha (jawless fishes). Lampreys are often parasitic, attaching to other fish with their sucker-like mouth, while hagfishes are primarily scavengers.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, what is the minimum age prescribed for a person to be a member of a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992, which granted constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions, lays down the qualifications for membership in a Panchayat. Article 243F(1)(a) of the Constitution of India specifies that a person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of a Panchayat if he is so disqualified by or under any law for the time being in force for the purposes of elections to the Legislature of the State concerned. However, it provides a crucial condition: "provided that no person shall be disqualified on the ground that he is less than twenty-five years of age, if he has attained the age of twenty-one years." This provision effectively sets the minimum age for contesting an election to a Panchayat at 21 years.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement about National Rural Livelihood Mission: a. It was launched in 2013 by the Ministry of Rural Development b. It was aided by the World Bank and IMF. c. It aims to create an efficient and effective system for the rural poor to access financial services. d. It was renamed as Deendayal Antayodaya Yojana (DAY-NRLM) in 2021.
questions.explanation: · National Rural Livelihood Mission (NRLM) was launched in 2011 by the Ministry of Rural Development and aided by the World Bank. · NRLM aims to create an efficient and effective system for the rural poor to access financial services. · In 2015, the program was renamed to Deendayal Antayodaya Yojana (DAY-NRLM). · This scheme also aims to attain access to rights, public services and other entitlements in rural areas. · The scheme has started Self Help Groups covering more than 7 crore households. · Sub Schemes under NRLM are- o Aajeevika Grameen Express Yojna o Mahila Kisan Sashatikaran Pariyojana o Startup Village Entrepreneurship programme o National Rural Livelihood Project

questions.question: Which of the following statement/s is/are correct regarding the AMRUT scheme? I. Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) was launched on 25th June 2015 in selected 500 cities and towns across the country. II. The Mission focuses on development of basic infrastructure, in the selected cities and towns, in the sectors of water supply; sewerage and septage management; storm water drainage; green spaces and parks; and non-motorized urban transport. III. AMRUT 2.0 scheme, has been launched on 01 October, 2021 for the period of 05 years i.e. from the financial year 2021-22 to the financial year 2025-26.
questions.explanation: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) was launched on 25th June 2015 in selected 500 cities and towns across the country. The Mission focuses on development of basic infrastructure, in the selected cities and towns, in the sectors of water supply; sewerage and septage management; storm water drainage; green spaces and parks; and non-motorized urban transport. Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) 2.0 scheme, which has been launched on 01 October, 2021 for the period of 05 years i.e. from the financial year 2021-22 to the financial year 2025-26, is designed to provide universal coverage of water supply through functional taps to all households in all the statutory towns in the country and coverage of sewerage/septage management in 500 cities covered in first phase of the AMRUT scheme. In news: As of May 19, 2024, a total of ₹83,357 crore has been disbursed under the AMRUT scheme.

questions.question: Which of the following subjects is NOT covered under the Concurrent List in the Seventh Schedule?
questions.explanation: · Option C (Public order) is not in the Concurrent List; it falls under the State List. · Education (Option A) is a Concurrent subject under Entry 25 of the Seventh Schedule. · Forests (Option B) appear in the Concurrent List under Entry 17. · Family welfare (Option D) is also Concurrent under Entry 6. · Concurrent List enables both Union and state to legislate on shared subjects. · State List exclusively empowers states on public order, police, and other local matters.

questions.question: In basketball, if the referee places one hand behind their head to form a triangle with their arm, it indicates
questions.explanation: Below is a list of the most common referee signals in basketball: Blocking: Referee places both hands on their hips. Carrying: Referee will repeatedly turn their palm over, alternating facing upwards then downwards. Charging: Referee places one hand behind their head to form a triangle with their arm. They will then point the opposite direction of the team that committed the foul. Double Dribble: Referee extends both hands outwards with palms facing down, and alternates moving them up and down as if to dribble a basketball. Holding: Referee forms a fist pointing upwards with one hand and grabs their forearm with the other, out in front of their body. Jump Ball: Referee lifts both arms up in front of their body with their thumbs up. Personal Foul: Referee raises one arm in a closed fist. Points Scored: Referee lifts one arm with the number of fingers held up denoting the number of points scored (two or three fingers) . Technical Foul: Referee puts both hands together perpendicularly to form a "T" shape. Three-Point Attempt: Referee extends one hand outward with three fingers raised.

questions.question: Assertion (A): The leeward side of mountains are relatively drier. Reason (R): High Mountains acts as the barrier for cold or hot winds.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: High mountains act as barriers for cold or hot winds; they may also cause precipitation if they are high enough and lie in the path of rain-bearing winds. The leeward side of mountains remains relatively dry.

questions.question: Which of the following iron ore belts is known for very high-grade hematites found in the Bailadila range of hills in the Bastar district of Chhattisgarh, with ore from these mines being exported to Japan and South Korea via Vishakhapatnam port?
questions.explanation: The Bailadila mines in the Durg-Bastar-Chandrapur iron ore belt (Chhattisgarh/Maharashtra) are renowned for: · High-grade hematite (over 65% iron content). · Exports to Japan/South Korea via Vishakhapatnam port. · Key mines: Bailadila-14 (NMDC-operated), Dalli-Rajhara. Bailadila's name means "Bull's Hump" (from local tribal language), reflecting the hill's shape.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true with respect to five-year plans? (A) The fourth five-year plan run from 1969-74 with actual growth of 3.3%. (B) The main objective of fifth year plan was 'Garibi Hatao' and 'Attainment of Self Reliance'. Select the correct answer using the codes given below
questions.explanation: o the details of fourth and fifth five-year plans is as below- Fourth Five Year PlanFifth Five Year Plan · Time period - 1969-74 · Target Growth- 5.7% · Actual Growth-3.3% · Hence statement A is correct. · Main emphasis was on growth rate of agriculture to enable other sectors to move forward. · First two years of the plan saw record production. · The last three years did not measure up due to poor monsoon. Implementation of Family Planning Programmes were amongst major targets of the Plan. · Time period- 1974-79 · Target Growth - 4.4% · Actual Growth - 4.8% · It proposed to achieve two main objectives: 'removal of poverty' (Garibi Hatao) and 'attainment of self-reliance'. · Hence statement 2 is correct. · Due to high inflation, cost calculations for the Plan proved to be completely wrong and the original public sector outlay had to be revised upwards. · After promulgation of emergency in 1975, the emphasis shifted to the implementation of Prime Ministers 20 Point Programme. FYP was relegated to the background and when Janta Party came to power in 1978, the Plan was terminated.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about glacial landforms: STATEMENT 1: When two cirques are carved back-to-back, the ridge between them becomes a sharp, knife-like edge called an Arête. STATEMENT 2: If three or more cirques erode a single mountain peak from different sides, they create a sharp, pyramid-shaped peak known as a Horn. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: An Arête is formed when two cirques erode a ridge from opposite sides, leaving a sharp, narrow crest. A Horn is formed when three or more cirques erode a mountain peak from different directions, producing a pyramidal peak. Two glaciers cut → sharp ridge Many glaciers cut → pointed mountain Further Insights Cirques are bowl-shaped depressions formed at the head of glaciers. Arêtes often form watershed boundaries between valleys. Horns are common in young fold mountains like the Alps and Himalayas. The Matterhorn is a classic example of a horn. Glacial landforms indicate past climatic conditions.

questions.question: According to Article 57, how many times can a person be re-elected as the President of India?
questions.explanation: • Article 57 places no restriction on the number of terms a person can be re-elected as President. Further Insight: • The President ○ Article 52 - The President of India. ○ Article 53 - Executive power of the Union is vested in the President. ○ Article 54 - Election of the President. ○ Article 55 - Manner of election of the President. ○ Article 56 - Term of office of the President (5 years). ○ Article 57 - Eligibility for re-election. ○ Article 58 - Qualifications for election as President. ○ Article 59 - Conditions of the President's office. ○ Article 60 - Oath or affirmation by the President. ○ Article 61 - Procedure for impeachment of the President. ○ Article 62 - Time of holding an election to fill a vacancy in the office of President and the term of office of a person elected to fill a casual vacancy.

questions.question: Which Union Minister unveiled the draft 'Shram Shakti Niti 2025' in October 2025?
questions.explanation: The draft National Labour and Employment Policy - Shram Shakti Niti 2025 was unveiled by the Union Minister of Labour & Employment, Mansukh Mandaviya, in October 2025 as part of efforts to reform labour policy and prepare the workforce for future jobs. Further insights: The policy aims to create an inclusive, future-ready labour ecosystem with universal social security. It envisions a shift from regulation to employment facilitation through digital public infrastructure. Shram Shakti Niti integrates existing labour codes and social security schemes into a unified framework

questions.question: Which of the following statements captures the theme of by 'The Covenant of Water' by Abraham Verghese?
options: It is a multigenerational family saga that examines love, faith, medicine, and a mysterious relationship with water in Kerala.
The novel primarily explores political upheavals in colonial India through the rise of nationalist movements.
The story focuses on urban life in modern India and the challenges of technological progress.
The novel is a historical account of maritime trade and naval expeditions along the Indian coastline.
questions.explanation: The Covenant of Water is a sweeping multigenerational family saga set in Kerala, blending themes of love, faith, medicine, destiny, and a recurring connection with water. The Covenant of Water is a 2023 novel by physician and author Abraham Verghese. The book tells the story of a Malayali family living in southwest India, in the State of Kerala, with the narrative spanning three generations, from 1900 to 1977. In each generation, some members of the family die by drowning because of an affliction they refer to as "the Condition".

questions.question: Which of the following are members of 15 naturally occurring metallic chemical elements whose atomic numbers are between 57 and 71?
questions.explanation: The members of the 15 naturally occurring metallic chemical elements whose atomic numbers are between 57 and 71 are known as the Lanthanides. The 15 naturally occurring metallic elements are with atomic numbers ranging from 57 (Lanthanum) to 71 (Lutetium). These elements are characterized by their similar properties and are often found together in nature. Alkali Metals: This group includes the elements in Group 1 of the periodic table, which are Lithium (Li), Sodium (Na), Potassium (K), Rubidium (Rb), Cesium (Cs), and Francium (Fr). Alkali metals are known for being highly reactive, especially with water, and they have one electron in their outermost shell. Halogens: Halogens are located in Group 17 of the periodic table and include Fluorine (F), Chlorine (Cl), Bromine (Br), Iodine (I), and Astatine (At). They are known for their high reactivity, particularly with alkali metals, and they tend to form salts when they react with metals. Actinides: The actinides are a series of 15 elements in the periodic table from atomic numbers 89 (Actinium) to 103 (Lawrencium). These elements are all radioactive and include well-known ones like Uranium (U) and Plutonium (Pu). Actinides are typically used in nuclear applications and are found below the lanthanides in the f-block of the periodic table.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are true about the Chekor festival? I.The Chekor (Parikrama of Wisdom) festival is celebrated in Shergaon, Arunachal Pradesh. II. It has been said that Gelong Doyan Tenzing, a monk introduced this festival in the Sherdukpen region. III. Chekor is the festival celebrated by Shertukpens.
questions.explanation: · The Chekor (Parikrama of Wisdom) festival is celebrated in Shergaon, Arunachal Pradesh. · It has been said that Gelong Doyan Tenzing, a monk introduced this festival in the Sherdukpen region. · The main theme of the day long festival portrays the introduction of Chhey/Holy ures of Buddhism. · Chekor is the festival celebrated by Shertukpens. · The festival is of a month long, filled with activities throughout the month. This festival acts as coaching time for younger generations to know about their culture. · This festival also tells a lot about how to behave and conduct the day-to-day activities of an adult villager. · This festival is observed during the month of May-June. The fourth month of lunar calendar called Jipa SakaDawa. · It starts with reciting holy scripts of Buddhist scared text. · The village council (blu) hires monks from nearby villages and monasteries for the recitation. The monks recite the scripts whole day till a day before poornima (full moon) day

questions.question: Which ISRO launch vehicle is the heavy-lift rocket used for Chandrayaan-2 and selected for Gaganyaan missions?
questions.explanation: LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk III) is ISRO's heavy-lift vehicle for high-mass payloads (e.g., Chandrayaan-2, Chandrayaan-3) and crewed Gaganyaan missions. PSLV and SSLV are for lighter payload classes; GSLV Mk II is medium-lift with indigenous cryogenic upper stage. Further Insights: - LVM3 can lift about 8 t to LEO and ~4 t to GTO-class orbits. - Chandrayaan-3 flew on LVM3-M4 (July 2023). - SSLV-D2 achieved success in 2023 for small satellites. - NISAR (NASAISRO SAR mission) is a major upcoming Earth observation mission using a US launcher.

questions.question: What was Mahatma Gandhi's initial involvement in India's freedom struggle after his return in 1915?
options: Directly leading a nationwide movement against the Rowlatt Act.
Becoming the undisputed leader of the National Movement immediately.
Involvement in local movements at Champaran, Kheda, and Ahmedabad.
Forming the Swaraj Party with Motilal Nehru and C.R. Das.
questions.explanation: After returning to India from South Africa in 1915, Gandhi's initial focus was on grassroots activism through three key local movements: · Champaran Satyagraha (1917): Advocated for indigo farmers against exploitative British planters. · Kheda Satyagraha (1918): Supported peasants during a famine-induced tax crisis. · Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918): Mediated labor disputes for textile workers.

questions.question: Which of the following postulates of Dalton's atomic theory explains the Law of Conservation of Mass?
options: Atoms combine in simple whole-number ratios.
Atoms are indivisible in a chemical reaction.
Atoms of different elements have different masses.
Atoms of the same element are identical in mass and properties.
questions.explanation: • Dalton's postulate that atoms are indivisible in a chemical reaction directly explains the Law of Conservation of Mass, as atoms cannot be destroyed or created, only rearranged. • Atoms combine in simple whole-number ratios: It explains the law of definite proportions. • Atoms of the same element are identical in mass and properties: It shows the consistency within an element.. Further Insight: • Dalton's Atomic Theory • Matter consists of small and indivisible particles called atoms. • Atoms of a given element are identical in mass and properties. • Atoms combine in simple whole-number ratios to form compounds(law of definite proportions). • Atoms cannot be created or destroyed in chemical reactions (supporting the Law of Conservation of Mass).

questions.question: Who designed and built the "Notre Dame des Anges" in Puducherry in 1855?
questions.explanation: Louis Guerre, a French engineer, was the main architect responsible for the design and construction of Notre Dame des Anges in 1855. He built the church using a Greco-Roman architectural style and employed techniques like lime mortar mixed with egg white, which helped the structure withstand coastal conditions. François Martin - first Governor-General of French Pondicherry in the 17th century. Napoleon III - He was the French Emperor (1852 - 1870) but was not involved in the church's architecture. Joseph François Dupleix - He was the Governor-General of French India famous for his conflict with the British. (1742 - 1754) Further Insights The construction material included egg white mixed with lime, providing long-term durability. It houses a rare statue of Mother Mary holding infant Jesus, donated from France. The church faces the Bay of Bengal, making it a landmark of French coastal architecture in India.

questions.question: Who is the current Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and Secretary of the Department of Space (DoS) as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Dr. V. Narayanan assumed charge on 14 January 2025 as Chairman of ISRO and Secretary of the Department of Space. S. Somanath - served as ISRO Chairman and Secretary, DoS from January 2022 until January 2025 K. Sivan - served earlier as Chairman of ISRO and Secretary, DoS from 2018 to 2022. A.S. Kiran Kumar - served as ISRO Chairman and Secretary, DoS prior to K. Sivan (2015 - 2018) Further insights V. Narayanan is a distinguished rocket propulsion expert who led the Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre (LPSC) before becoming ISRO Chairman. S. Somanath's tenure included major missions like Chandrayaan-3 and Aditya-L1 before handing over leadership. K. Sivan oversaw the Chandrayaan-2 mission during his tenure.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Gross National Product (GNP) (A) It is the GDP of a country added with its 'income from abroad'. (B) It represents only the quantitative aspects of the economy. (C) It is taken as the 'National Income' (NI) of an economy. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Ø Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the value of the all final goods and services produced within the boundary of a nation during one year period. For India, this calendar year is from 1st April to 31st March. Ø It is a 'quantitative' concept and its volume/size indicates the 'internal' strength of the economy. But it does not say anything about the 'qualitative' aspects of the goods and services produced. Ø Gross National Product (GNP) is the GDP of a country added with its 'income from abroad'. Here, the trans-boundary economic activities of an economy are also taken into account. Ø It is a more exhaustive concept of national income than the GDP as it indicates towards the 'quantitative' as well as the 'qualitative' aspects of the economy, i.e., the 'internal' as well as the 'external' strength of the economy. Ø Net National Product (NNP) of an economy is the GNP after deducting the loss due to 'depreciation'. This is the 'National Income' (NI) of an economy.

questions.question: India and the Maldives conduct an annual joint military exercise to enhance interoperability and counter-terrorism capabilities. What is the name of this exercise?
questions.explanation: Exercise Ekuverin is a bilateral military exercise conducted annually between: Indian Army Maldives National Defence Force (MNDF) The term "Ekuverin" means "Friends" in the Dhivehi language. The exercise focuses on: Counter-terrorism operations Humanitarian assistance Interoperability between forces Samudra Shakti is a naval exercise between India and Indonesia. Surya Kiran is a joint military exercise between India and Nepal. It focuses on mountain warfare and disaster response. Nomadic Elephant is conducted between India and Mongolia. It focuses on counter-insurgency training. Further Insights Maldives is a key partner in India's Neighbourhood First Policy. India supports Maldives under SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region). Military exercises strengthen defence diplomacy.

questions.question: Arrange the following in the correct sequence of daily parliamentary proceedings: A) Zero Hour begins B) Question Hour concludes C) Question Hour begins D) Regular Business of the House
questions.explanation: The day typically starts with Question Hour (60 minutes), followed by Zero Hour (for raising matters of public importance), then adjournment motions if any, and finally the regular business of the house. Explanation of the Sequence: C) Question Hour begins: The first hour of a sitting of Parliament is the Question Hour. It typically starts at 11:00 AM. During this time, Members of Parliament (MPs) ask questions to ministers about various aspects of their ministries and government administration. B) Question Hour concludes: The Question Hour lasts for one hour and concludes at 12:00 PM. A) Zero Hour begins: Immediately following the Question Hour, at 12:00 PM, the Zero Hour commences. This is the time when MPs can raise matters of urgent public importance without any prior notice. D) Regular Business of the House: After the Zero Hour (and usually after a lunch break), the House takes up the formal, listed agenda for the day. This is referred to as the regular business and includes legislative activities such as the introduction of bills, discussions on motions, and debates on various national issues. Further Insights: Question Hour: This is a critical instrument of parliamentary oversight, holding the executive accountable for its actions and policies. Zero Hour: This is an Indian parliamentary innovation and is not mentioned in the Rules of Procedure. It provides a platform for MPs to raise pressing issues spontaneously, though the Speaker/Chairman has the discretion to allow or disallow such matters. The exact timings and order can sometimes be adjusted based on the specific circumstances of the day or the House.

questions.question: Who won in the final of the 2024-25 Ranji Trophy to become the sixth team to lift the trophy on three or more occasions?
questions.explanation: Vidarbha beat Kerala in the final of the 2024-25 Ranji Trophy to become the sixth team to lift the trophy on three or more occasions. The 2024-25 Ranji Trophy also known as the IDFC First Bank Ranji Trophy for sponsorship reasons, was the 90th season of the Ranji Trophy taking place from 11 October to 26 February 2025. Mumbai were the defending champions.

questions.question: In the following figure, 'O' is the centre of the circle. What is the measure of ∠BAC? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6824/image003.jpg)
questions.explanation: Joining OC: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6824/image004.jpg) OB = OC = radius So, ∠OBC = ∠OCB = 48° So, ∠BOC = 180 - 48 - 48 = 84° So, ∠BAC = (½)∠BOC = 42°

questions.question: A triangle MNO is such that MN = NO = OM. From M a perpendicular MP is drawn. Which of the following is equal to MP?
questions.explanation: We know that perpendicular height of an equilateral triangle = (√3/2) * side of triangle. So, MP = (√3/2) * MN

questions.question: A rectangular piece of paper 14 cm X 11 cm is folded without overlapping to make a cylinder of height 14 cm. Find the volume of the cylinder.
questions.explanation: As per the given condition, perimeter of the curved surface of the cylinder = 11 2πr = 11 => r = 11 x 7/(22 x 2) = 1.75 cm Volume = 22/7 x 1.75 x 1.75 x 14 = 134.75 cm³

questions.question: Find the value of the expression: A² - 1_________A + 1 - A² - 16_________A + 4
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22721/image001.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22721/image002.png) = A - 1 - (A - 4) = A - 1 - A + 4 = 3

questions.question: A took a loan of Rs. 5000 from B at 5% rate of simple interest and B give the total amount received after 2 years to C at 10% rate of simple interest for 3 years. Find the total interest amount earned by B.
questions.explanation: Interest earned after 2 years = (5000 * 5 * 2)/100 = Rs. 500 Amount after 2 years = 5000 + 500 = 5500 Interest earned from C = (5500 * 10 * 3)/100 = Rs. 1650 Amount after 5 years = 5500 + 1650 = Rs. 7150 Total interest = Rs. (7150 - 5000) = Rs. 2150

questions.question: For three numbers - x, y and z, x is 2 times of y and 3 times of z. If their average is 77/3, then what is the value of x?
Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct? I: ⅝ < 21/32 < 45/64 II: 8/9 < 17/21 < 11/15
Which of the following is the greatest? 2 : 5, 1 : 2, 8 : 15, 3 : 5
questions.explanation: Let us take LCM of second terms of each ratio. LCM of 5, 2, 15, and 5 = 30 The ratios can now be written as- 12:30, 15:30, 16:30 and 18:30 The greatest of the above ratios is 18:30 Hence, the greatest ratio is 3:5

questions.question: Find the length of longest rod that can be placed in the room of dimension 10*12*15 metres.
If tanθ = 21/20, then what is the value of (3cosθ - 2sinθ)?
questions.explanation: tanθ = 21/20 Perpendicular = 21, Base = 20 Hypotenuse = √(21² + 20²) = √(441 + 400) = 29 Now, 3cosθ - 2sinθ = 3*20/29 - 2*21/29 = 18/29

questions.question: The pie chart shows the result (in percentage) of a survey about the medium of transport people use to reach their office every morning. Total number of people surveyed is 600. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21654/image001.png) The ratio of number of females to that of males who go to office by car is 1: 5 and the ratio of number of females to that of males who go to office by Metro is 2: 3. Find the difference between the number of females who go to the office by car and by metro.
questions.explanation: Number of people who go to office by car = 600 * 11/100 = 66 Number of females who go to office by car = 66 * ⅙ = 11 Number of people who go to office by Metro = 600 * 10/100 = 60 Number of females who go to office by Metro = 60 * ⅖ = 24 Required difference = 24 - 11 = 13

questions.question: Alok purchased 25 Cadbury candies at Rs. 20 and sold 20 Cadbury candies at Rs. 25, then what is the profit or loss percentage earned by him?
questions.explanation: Cost price of 25 Cadbury candies = Rs. 20 And cost price of 1 Cadbury candy = 20/25 = Rs. 0.8 And Selling price of 20 Cadbury candies = Rs. 25 So, selling price of 1 Cadbury candy = 25/20 = Rs. 1.25 Profit = 1.25 - 0.8 = Rs. 0.45 Hence, profit percentage = (0.45/0.8) * 100 = 56.25%

questions.question: Machine A can print a book in 24 min, machine B is 25% less efficient than A. With the help of machine C, all the three machines together can print that book in 8 min. Find the time taken by machine C alone to print that book.
questions.explanation: B is 25% less efficient than A which means time taken by A alone is 25% less than B. Time taken by B alone to print the book = 24 * (100/75) = 32 min Let time taken by C alone to print the book = 't' min According to the question: (1/24) + (1/32) + (1/t) = (⅛) (1/t) = (⅛) - (1/24) - (1/32) = (12 - 4 - 3)/96 t = 96/5 t = 19.2 min

questions.question: Three racing bikes start together on a 240 m track with speeds 6 m/s, 8 m/s, and 10 m/s in the same direction. After how many seconds will the fastest be exactly two laps ahead of the slowest when crossing the starting line?
questions.explanation: Length of the track = 240 m Speeds of the bikes = 6 m/s, 8 m/s and 10 m/s Speed of the slowest bike = 6 m/s Speed of the fastest bike = 10 m/s To be two laps ahead, the fastest bike must gain 2 240 = 480 m over the slowest bike Relative speed of the fastest bike with respect to the slowest bike = 10 − 6 = 4 m/s Time taken = Distance gained / Relative speed = 480 / 4 = 120 seconds

questions.question: Two trains start at the same time from stations A and B which are 300 km apart, running towards each other at 50 km/h and 70 km/h respectively. How far from station A do they meet?
questions.explanation: Time to meet = 300/(50 + 70) = 300/120 = 2.5 h Distance from A = 50 X 2.5 = 125 km

questions.question: Simran can do a 40% of a work in 18 days and Reyansh can do the whole work in 20 days then, in how many days working together they can finish (13/15)th part of work?
questions.explanation: Simran can do 40% of work in 18 days So, total work will be completed in = 18 * 5/2 = 45 days Let total work = 180 units (LCM of 45 and 20) Efficiency of Simran = 180/45 = 4 unit/day Efficiency of Reyansh = 180/20 = 9 unit/day Total efficiency of Simran and Reyansh in one day = (4 + 9) = 13 unit/day Required time = (180/13) * (13/15) = 12 days

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle ABC, AD is a median and G is the centroid. If GD = 3√3 cm, then what is the area of triangle ABC?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3792/image006.jpg) GD = 3√3 cm AD = 3√3 * (3/1) = 9√3 cm Side of triangle = (2/√3) * median = (2/√3) * 9√3 = 18 cm Area = (√¾) x 18 x 18 = 81√3 sq.cm

questions.question: The quantity of honey is ⅜ times the quantity of milk. If 40 ml of milk and 55 ml of honey are added to the mixture, the ratio becomes 16:7. Find the initial quantity of honey.
questions.explanation: The initial quantity of milk = 8n ml The initial quantity of honey = 3n ml Now, (8n + 40)/(3n + 55) = 16/7 7(n + 5) = 2(3n + 55) 7n + 35 = 6n + 110 n = 75 The initial quantity of honey = 3 X 75 = 225 ml

questions.question: If the radius of the circle X² + Y² - 12X + 16Y + C = 0 is 9, then find the value of C.
Which of the following given values is greater than 10¹/⁴
questions.explanation: ⁴√10 = 10¹/⁴ and 4¹/³ : (10¹/⁴)¹² =10³ = 1000 and (4¹/³)¹² = 4⁴ = 256 Hence, 4¹/³ is smaller than 10¹/⁴ (60¹/⁴)⁴ = 60 is greater than (10¹/⁴)⁴ = 10, Hence 60¹/⁴ is greater than 10¹/⁴. (2¹/²)⁴ is smaller than (10¹/⁴)⁴ (900¹/¹²)¹² is smaller than (10¹/⁴)¹²

questions.question: If m = √3/2, then the value of √(1+m)+ √(1-m)

What grammatical function does the clause "that the Pink Tax places an unfair financial burden on women" serve in the sentence below? "While critics argue that the Pink Tax places an unfair financial burden on women, defenders claim that price variations arise from legitimate economic factors rather than bias."
questions.explanation: The clause "that the Pink Tax places an unfair financial burden on women" functions as the object of the verb "argue." It begins with the subordinating conjunction "that" and acts as a noun within the sentence. Therefore, it is a noun clause. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the word closest in meaning to the word "systematic" as used in the passage.
questions.explanation: In the passage, "systematic" refers to pricing patterns that are structured and recurring as a result of gendered marketing strategies. Among the options, "organised" best captures this sense of regularity and planning, while the other options suggest chance or impermanence. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word "oversimplification" as used in the context "the Pink Tax is an oversimplification of complex pricing mechanisms"?
questions.explanation: The term "oversimplification" implies presenting a complex issue as simpler than it actually is. In this context, critics argue that pricing cannot be explained by gender bias alone. Therefore, "reduction of complexity" best conveys the intended meaning. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the overall tone of the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage presents both sides of the Pink Tax debate, weighing arguments and counterarguments without adopting a strongly emotional or sarcastic stance. It evaluates evidence and arrives at a balanced conclusion. Hence, the tone is best described as neutral and evaluative. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the tense and voice used in the following sentence taken from the passage: "Items such as razors, deodorants, and shampoos often differ only in colour, fragrance, or packaging, yet are priced higher when labelled for women."
questions.explanation: The sentence contains two verb phrases. The verb "differ" is in the present simple active voice, as the subject "items" performs the action of differing. The verb phrase "are priced" is in the present simple passive voice, where the subject receives the action of pricing. Since both active and passive constructions occur within the same sentence in the present simple tense, the correct option is Present Simple (Active and Passive). Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. They describe stories of their visits and they are told that it was due to stress, anxiety, or depression. B. At times, they are also told it is because of their age or even their weight. C. I regularly hear from patients that they have seen multiple physicians while trying to get a diagnosis or an understanding of why they don't feel well. D. Their experience with these physicians usually leaves them feeling sad and frustrated, as well as doubting themselves.
questions.explanation: C must be the introductory sentence of the passage. C talks about people's experiences with different physicians and D must follow C. D refers to the physicians mentioned in C using the pronoun 'these'. D talks about the experience that patients have with these physicians and AB describe those experiences in detail. A mentions that the patients describe stories of their visits and B elaborates on it. Hence, d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. They are even deemed a threat to the official currency and monetary system. Q. As they need neither a central issuing authority nor these currencies can exist and thrive outside the realm of authority and regulation. R. The question then is whether banning cryptocurrencies is the most effective way to respond. S. Governments and economic regulators across the world are wary of private cryptocurrencies.
questions.explanation: S should come first, as it introduces us to the situation - mistrust about cryptocurrencies. Q follows, telling us the unique features of cryptocurrencies. P comes next, saying that they are a threat to the established system; this can be the reason behind the wariness mentioned in S. R comes last, posing the question of whether banning cryptocurrencies is the right response. SQPR is the correct sequence. Hence, option b) is correct.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Leaders can succeed in this only when they can cut through differences of race, sex, age, nationality, and personality and, using stories and celebrating heroes, tap into those very few needs we all share. B. On the other hand, the job of a manager is to turn one person's particular talent into performance. C. The job of a great leader is to rally people toward a better future by discovering what is universal and capitalizing on it. D. Managers will succeed only when they can identify and deploy the differences among people, challenging each employee to excel in his or her own way.
questions.explanation: The correct order is CABD. Both CA & BD form mandatory pairs. CA form a mandatory pair because C describes the responsibilities of a great leader, while A mentions how the leaders are able to succeed in discharging them. BD form a mandatory pair because B mentions what the job of a manager is, while D mentions how managers successfully do their job. Now, the use of 'on the other hand' in statement B necessitates that statement B will always come after statement C. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The indentured labourers fix the roads.
options: The roads was fixed by indentured labourers.
The roads were fixed by indentured labourers.
The roads are fixed by indentured labourers.
The roads were being fixed by indentured labourers.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of the verb [are] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Present Indefinite. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Present Indefinite. The format of Active voice is: Subject + V1/V5 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + am/is/are + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: She likes you. (Active Voice) You are liked by her. (Passive Voice) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (c): The roads are fixed by indentured labourers.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words/phrases. An official order forbidding something.
questions.explanation: 'Prohibition' means an official order forbidding something. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. Eulogy: A speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly, typically someone who has just died. Perennial: Lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time; enduring or continually recurring. Panacea: A solution or remedy for all difficulties or diseases.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence. The CEO's magnanimity towards his competitors earned him widespread respect in the industry.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate antonym for the word magnanimity is meanness. Magnanimity means generosity and nobility of spirit, especially towards rivals or less powerful individuals. In the sentence, it refers to the CEO's generous and forgiving nature. Meanness means a lack of generosity or spitefulness, which is the opposite. Generosity and benevolence are synonyms of magnanimity, while harshness is unrelated but not an opposite in meaning here. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. A ceremony for the competition ______________ between the teams.
questions.explanation: The subject of the sentence is 'ceremony' (a formal religious or public occasion, especially one celebrating a particular event, achievement, or anniversary), which is singular in nature. So, we cannot use the helping verb 'have' in the sentence as it is used with plural nouns. So, option (a) can be eliminated. The sentence lays emphasis on the receiver of the action. So, the sentence should be in a passive voice. So, we can eliminate options (b) and (d) as they are in active voice. The structure of the passive voice is 'has/had/have + been + past participle verb (V3)'. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: A ceremony for the competition has been performed between the teams. Note: Voice of a sentence - Depending upon the emphasis we want to lay we use a particular voice in a particular context. We use active voice to lay emphasis on the doer of the action while we use passive voice to lay emphasis on the receiver of the action. Example: Active- Radha will work. (emphasis on Radha) Passive- Work will be done by Radha. (emphasis on Work)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The sudden wind made the candles that I decorated _________.
questions.explanation: Let's look at the meanings of the given phrasal verbs. break out: spread (war, epidemic, fire, riots). break up: terminate (meeting, school, session). blow out: extinguish. blow up: explode, start suddenly. Among the given phrasal verbs, 'blow out' is the only phrasal verb that fits correctly in the sentence. It implies that the sudden breeze caused the flame of the candles to extinguish. The complete sentence is: The sudden wind made the candles that I decorated blow out. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The king asked the stranger why he had come through flame and snow to visit their halls.
options: The king said to the stranger, "Why you come through flame and snow to visit our halls?"
The king asked the stranger, "Why you did come through flame and snow to visit our halls?"
The king said to the stranger, "Why have you come through flame and snow to visit our halls?"
The king asked the stranger, "Why you have come through flame and snow to visit our halls?"
questions.explanation: In the case of Interrogative sentences like this, "enquired (of)", "wanted to know", "asked", etc in Indirect Speech is changed into "said" in Direct Speech. In Direct speech, the verb is always placed before the subject, unlike in Indirect Speech. For example: The teacher asked him what his name was. (Indirect Speech) The teacher said to him, "What is your name?" (Direct Speech) The tense of the Indirect Speech is in the Past Perfect. So, the tense in the Direct Speech will be in the Present Perfect. Though, there are two other cases for the conversion of sentence in past perfect tense into indirect speech [Tense remains same; or Past Perfect changes to Simple Past]. But, as per the options, Past Perfect will change to Present Perfect. The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: · The reporting verb 'asked' is changed into 'said to'. · The verb 'he had come' is changed into 'have you come'. · 'Their halls' is changed into 'our halls'. Therefore, the correct answer is option (c): The king said to the stranger, "Why have you come through flame and snow to visit our halls?"

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Baffled
questions.explanation: 'Baffled' means '(Adj) Extremely confused or puzzled'. Options a) and d) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Puzzled' means 'Unable to understand; perplexed'. Hence, option b) can also be rejected as it has the same meaning as the word. 'Devious' means 'Showing a skilful use of underhand tactics to achieve goals'. 'Fragile' means '(of an object) easily broken or damaged'. Hence, option c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the meaning of the idiom in bold and italics. For straining every nerve, he must get something worthy.
questions.explanation: The meaning of the idiom 'strain every nerve' is 'make every possible effort'. Among the options, 'making every possible effort' is the correct option that matches the meaning of the given idiom. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who writes shorthand.
questions.explanation: 'Stenographer' means someone skilled in the transcription of speech. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. 'Stationer' means one who sells paper, ink, pens, etc. 'Plumassier' means one who works or deals in feathers for apparel. 'Veterinarian' means one skilled in the treatment of diseases of animals.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Erudite
questions.explanation: Erudite means having or showing great knowledge or learning; for example, "The erudite professor could answer any question on ancient history." Scholarly means involving or relating to serious academic study; for example, "She wrote a scholarly article on the subject." Both words describe a person or work characterized by deep knowledge and learning, making scholarly the correct synonym. The other options - Ignorant (lacking knowledge), Frivolous (not serious), and Impulsive (acting without forethought) are unrelated or opposite in meaning. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: The firm decided to expand into new markets.
options: There are expansion plans for the newly started firm.
The law firm handled complex cases efficiently.
Her handshake was firm and confident.
We approached a lawyer to fight the summon against our firm.
questions.explanation: Options a), b), and d) use "firm" in business or solid senses, but option c) uses "firm" as solid texture, which is unrelated to organization. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. After the building collapsed, it has been estimated that only a less people survived the disaster.
questions.explanation: The error is in Option (a). The determiner 'less' is used to modify only uncountable or immeasurable nouns such as money, food, emotions, etc. In this sentence, 'less' is used to modify a countable noun 'people', which is incorrect. Instead, 'few people' will be correct. Hence, the correct answer is option (a). Note:- Determiner: A modifying word that determines the kind of reference a noun or noun group has, for example, 'a', 'the', 'every', 'much', 'many', etc.

questions.question: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose "No Error". The blind person has shown incredible resilience and adaptability in navigating the challenges of everyday life.
options: the blind person has shown incredible
questions.explanation: The given sentence is grammatically and contextually correct. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. If the shareholders don't agree to the proposition, it is us who will convince them.
questions.explanation: 'It + Be verb' (am, is, are, was, were, will be, have been, has been, had been) is always followed by a Subjective Pronoun. Thus, 'it is' must be followed by 'we'. Hence, the sentence needs an improvement. Thus, the given sentence and option (a) are incorrect. The sentence starts with 'If'. 'If' denotes that the given sentence is a conditional statement. If the condition is in the present tense (don't agree), then its result should be in the future (denoted by 'will'). So, option (b) is incorrect as 'would' is used in place of 'will'. Thus, 'we who will' should replace 'us who will'. Therefore, the correct answer is (c), we who will. The corrected form of the sentence is: If the shareholders don't agree to the proposition, it is we who will convince them. Additional Information: The three types of conditional sentences (with examples) are given in the table. IIf I study (V1)I will pass the exam IIIf I studied (V2)I would pass the exam IIIIf I had studied (had + V3)I would have passed the exam

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "DESTITUTE".
questions.explanation: The word "Destitute" means without basic necessities or extremely poor. 1) "Impoverished" - correct synonym; lacking financial resources. 2) "Affluent" - opposite; wealthy. 3) "Prosperous" - opposite; financially thriving. 4) "Opulent" - opposite; luxurious. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correctly spelt word. The journalist was known for his ___ commitment to factual accuracy, even under political pressure.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, scrupulous is correctly spelt and means showing great care and attention to moral correctness or accuracy. This fits the context of commitment to factual accuracy. Hence, (a) scrupulous is the correct answer. The remaining options are incorrect spellings.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option (d) is the correct answer. The correct spelling is "annihilate". The other options are spelled correctly. Acquire: to obtain or get something. Adjacent: next to or adjoining something. Animator: a person who creates animations or brings something to life. Annihilate: to destroy completely.
